Implemented only the Quick Metronome v0 contract scope: tempo, meter, subdivision, accent, countdown, tap tempo, metronome playback, quick recording, replay, quick session/recording metadata, persistence, and a minimal latest quick recording outlet.
Metronome UI calls a BrowserMetronomeService boundary that uses Web Audio scheduling; the UI does not directly call Web Audio scheduler APIs.

Recording UI calls a BrowserRecordingService boundary that uses MediaRecorder; E2E injects a controlled synthetic audio stream through getUserMedia and verifies a non-empty quick recording is saved and replayable.

Code review fix pass: BrowserMetronomeService now emits scheduled tick trace evidence with audioTime, BPM, expected interval, subdivision, and accent status so E2E can measure actual scheduled spacing instead of relying on UI text.

Code review fix pass: BrowserRecordingService now decodes the captured artifact after stop and records duration, sample rate, peak, RMS, estimated frequency, and silence status; E2E independently decodes the saved data URL and verifies the expected 440Hz synthetic input is present without obvious truncation or silence.
QA fix pass: BPM text input now keeps a draft value while typing and commits/clamps only on blur, Enter, stepper, or tap tempo, so values like 60 can be entered normally.
QA fix pass: time signature, subdivision, accent, and countdown are disabled with explanatory text while the metronome is running or counting down; BPM remains live-adjustable.
QA fix pass: empty latest-recording and recordings outlets now show a clearly marked playable demo synthetic 440Hz WAV sample that is not persisted or labeled as a user recording.

Review fix pass on staged QA diff: E2E now decodes the demo WAV data URL with browser AudioContext and verifies duration, RMS, peak, and estimated 440Hz frequency; unit tests also validate the RIFF/WAVE PCM header.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: E2E now covers BPM blur commit and draft-then-stepper behavior.

Review fix pass on staged QA diff: BrowserMetronomeService now uses a Tone.js adapter boundary with Tone.Transport scheduled callbacks and Tone.Synth triggerAttackRelease instead of hand-written Web Audio oscillator/setInterval scheduling.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: the Tone adapter stop path clears the scheduled event, stops/cancels Tone.Transport, and disposes the Synth to avoid global transport pollution.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: unit tests inject a fake Tone adapter to prove scheduled callbacks trigger the Tone instrument path and emit trace evidence with matching scheduled time.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 05e-session-integration.
Coding model used: GPT-5 Codex, no fast tier.

Implemented only the Sheet Practice session integration contract scope: sourceType sheet session creation/restoration, sheetId binding, basic duration, lastPracticedAt updates, recent/Continue Practice target, persistence, and invalid sheet guard.

Added a clearly scoped 05e Sheet Practice session harness because 05b/05c UI controls are not implemented yet; it emits high-level metronome and recording triggers without pretending those submodules are complete.
Recording trigger support creates only sheet recording metadata linked to sessionId and sheetId; it does not create recording audio artifacts or call MediaRecorder.
Metronome and recording trigger states remain independent at the session metadata boundary: each can start or stop without forcing the other state to change.
Sheet Practice UI talks to the practice-session service boundary and does not call Tone.js, MediaRecorder, Dexie, recording internals, or viewer/storage internals directly.
Home Continue Practice now reads the recent practice-session target and routes sheet sessions to /sheet-practice/[sheetId].
Review fix: Continue Practice now validates that the recent sheet session's sheet still exists before returning a Sheet Practice target, so stale sessions after sheet deletion are ignored.

Review fix: 05e sheet recording metadata now writes through the existing Recordings Review history boundary instead of a disconnected Dexie recordings table; the session Dexie schema migrates to sessions-only storage.

Review fix: metadata-only sheet recordings are visible through the shared /recordings history with no audio artifact, preserving the 05e no-audio constraint while giving 05c/03 one canonical metadata path.

Strict review fix: Home Continue Practice now uses a global practice-session repository adapter that includes existing Quick Metronome sessions from the shared recording history snapshot and sheet sessions from Dexie, with recency sorting across both.
Strict review fix: stopping the final active 05e trigger now persists endedAt and duration through an explicit endPracticeSession service operation.
Strict review fix: PracticeSession and SheetRecordingMetadata now have Zod schemas applied at save/load/metadata bridge boundaries to reject invalid sourceType/sheetId/date/duration combinations.

Strict review fix: Sheet Practice route construction is centralized in domain/sheet routes and reused by Home Continue, Sheet Library, and Recordings Review while preserving query route compatibility for recordingId links.
Strict review fix: Sheet Practice harness duration display now reuses the shared practice duration formatter instead of a local formatter.

Strict re-review fix: practice date validation now uses Zod's z.iso.datetime({ offset: true }) with a strict round-trip refinement for real ISO instants, covering non-ISO strings, impossible dates, and invalid timezone/format cases.
Strict re-review fix: the recording history metadata bridge validates PracticeSession before saving and enforces sheet sourceType plus matching recording/session and recording/sheet linkage.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because this status commit cannot contain its own final object hash.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 05b-practice-controls.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High, no fast tier.

Implemented only the Sheet Practice controls contract: bottom practice controls, metronome play/stop, BPM/time signature/subdivision/accent/countdown settings, visible playing/stopped state, sheet default initialization, session activity trigger, and responsive desktop/mobile layout.
Reused the verified 02 Quick Metronome BrowserMetronomeService, Tone adapter boundary, settings helpers, and scheduled tick trace event; no new scheduler, oscillator, or metronome service was added.
Reused the verified 05e browserPracticeSessionService ensureSheetSession/update/end APIs for sheet session activity; no parallel session path was added.
Starting the Sheet Practice metronome creates or restores a sheet session and updates sheet practice activity, but does not create recording artifacts or recording metadata.
Recording independence is covered through a small recording-active harness state inside the controls; it does not call MediaRecorder, Dexie recording writes, or sheet recording metadata creation.
Sheet Practice UI continues to stay behind service/hook boundaries and does not directly call Tone.js, Web Audio scheduler internals, MediaRecorder, Dexie, PDF internals, or image decoding internals.
Updated the previous 05e E2E browser path to use real 05b controls instead of the temporary session metronome trigger harness.

Strict review fix: Sheet Practice now ensures/restores the sheet session before starting Tone playback and still calls metronome stop on every failure path; unit coverage injects a rejecting session service and proves Tone start is not called and stop cleanup runs.

Strict review fix: visible fake Rec On/Rec Off production buttons were removed. Recording independence is now driven only by a test-injected harness event, with no recording UI/audio/metadata implemented for 05b.
Strict review fix: unsupported sheet meters such as 5/4 now show an explicit recoverable fallback message instead of silently discarding sheet metadata.
Strict review fix: browser timing coverage now selects Every beat and Off accents and verifies the changed accent trace patterns.
Strict review fix: shared BPM draft commit/step behavior was extracted into useMetronomeBpmDraft and reused by Quick Metronome and Sheet Practice controls.
Second strict review fix: shared transport/countdown/start/stop/update/cleanup behavior was extracted into useMetronomeTransport and reused by Quick Metronome and Sheet Practice controls.

Second strict review fix: if a sheet session is ensured but Tone startup rejects, Sheet Practice stops playback state and ends only metronome-only sessions; recording-linked sessions are deliberately left intact.

Third strict review fix: Sheet Practice metronome start now records explicit ownership by checking for an existing sheet session before ensureSheetSession; Tone-start failure only rolls back a session created by this metronome start, so existing sessions and active recording sessions without saved metadata are preserved.

Third strict review fix: Sheet Practice now locks meter, subdivision, countdown, and accent while running or counting down, while leaving BPM editable; browser coverage exercises both running and countdown lock states.

Fourth strict review fix: shared metronome transport now starts playback after countdown using the latest settings and callbacks, so BPM edits made during countdown drive the real Tone start settings, scheduled trace, and saved sheet session.

Fourth strict review fix: Sheet Practice start context now distinguishes created metronome-only sessions, preexisting active sessions, and previously ended sessions reopened by ensureSheetSession; Tone-start failure ends only created sessions and restores a reopened ended snapshot instead of leaving it active.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because this status commit cannot contain its own final object hash.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 05c-sheet-recording-review.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.
Implemented production Sheet Practice recording controls using service boundaries; UI does not directly call MediaRecorder, wavesurfer, raw Web Audio, IndexedDB, or localStorage.

Sheet recording capture reuses the Quick Metronome BrowserRecordingService and persists sheet recordings through the shared Recordings Review history boundary with real audio artifacts, artifact analysis, and trusted peaks derived after decoding the artifact.

Sheet Practice latest-take replay and waveform reuse the Recordings Review artifact loader and wavesurfer playback adapter; waveform peaks are inspected and remain stable across play/pause, repeated sampling, resize, and reload in E2E.
Recording and metronome controls remain independent: stopping recording does not stop the metronome, and stopping the metronome does not stop recording.

Practice Again from Recordings passes the source recording id into Sheet Practice; the next recording creates a fresh sheet session and a new recording id, while the source recording metadata and audio artifact remain unchanged.

Strict review fix: sheet recording now decodes the captured artifact and validates derived peaks before creating sheet recording metadata, so post-decode failure does not leave a metadata placeholder or session latestRecordingId.

Strict re-review fix: final shared history save failure now restores the previous shared history snapshot and restores or deletes the sheet practice session snapshot, so session recordingCount/latestRecordingId are not advanced without a saved artifact.

Strict review fix: trusted peaks now require finite, non-zero normalized values; invalid persisted peaks produce a clear artifact/waveform error, while missing trusted peaks still derive peaks from decoded audio.

Strict re-review fix: trusted peak arrays are checked for raw finite numeric values before normalization, preventing Infinity or other non-finite inputs from being normalized into an accepted waveform.

Strict review fix: Recordings Review and latest Sheet Practice replay share the same artifact review component for loading, waveform adapter setup, play/pause, derived peaks, source display, artifact errors, and duration mismatch warnings.
Added schedule-token and monotonic trigger-time guards to BrowserMetronomeService so stale or repeated Tone.Transport callback times cannot trigger synth scheduling errors.
No error markers, multi-take management, reference comparison, scoring, export, or v1 analysis behavior was implemented.

Implemented manual recording-scoped error markers for Sheet Practice latest recordings only; no automatic mistake detection, bar-aware markers, sheet overlays, categories, severity, scoring, or analysis behavior was added.

Markers require an existing recordingId, validate timestamp against recording duration, trim optional notes, persist in the shared recordings review storage shape, sort by timestamp, and can be deleted with deletion persisting after reload.
The marker panel shows a clear empty state when Sheet Practice has no latest sheet recording.
Clicking a marker calls the shared recording playback boundary seekToMs and Playwright verifies the real playback currentTime through the recordings-review:seek event, not list highlighting.

Strict review fix: repository read/write boundaries now normalize persisted markers with the shared marker validation path and filter orphaned, non-finite, negative, out-of-range, untrimmed-overlong, and otherwise invalid markers before they reach UI snapshots or storage.
Strict review fix: marker seek now returns a playback boundary result; Sheet Practice only shows success when currentTime is confirmed within tolerance and otherwise shows a recoverable error.
Strict review fix: long unbroken marker notes wrap in both Sheet Practice and Recordings Review, with Playwright mobile overflow coverage.

Recordings Review keeps using the shared artifact review component and marker display; the shared playback component now exposes reusable currentTime/seek controls without changing existing Recordings Review behavior.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 06-reference-system.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only the v0 Sheet Practice reference contract: reference panel, local audio upload/play/pause/volume, Bilibili search, selectable search result save, pasted Bilibili URL save, persistence by sheetId, reload restore, and clear error states.

Local audio references are saved with metadata plus a real Blob artifact in the reference repository; browser add flow decodes the uploaded file before saving and the playback boundary wraps HTMLAudioElement.

Bilibili search uses a deterministic adapter boundary for automated tests and stores display/reopen metadata plus an iframe embed URL or external link; it does not download video, extract audio, analyze media, sync, loop, change speed, or expose offset controls.
Reference playback triggers sheet session activity through the existing practice-session service with trigger reference and does not stop active metronome or recording state.

E2E uses a generated 1.2s 440Hz WAV payload uploaded through the visible Local audio file input, then verifies currentTime advance while playing, pause stability, volume propagation, and reload persistence.
E2E uses DeterministicBilibiliSearchAdapter fixture result Canon in D guitar practice reference / BV1ab411c7dE for visible search and result selection; URL paste uses BV1XY411P7mn.

Shared Sheet Practice viewer composition was touched only to mount the 06-owned reference panel beside the sheet and preserve the existing bottom controls; adjacent sheet viewer and controls E2E specs were rerun.

Strict review fix: removed the Bilibili iframe from the reference panel so deterministic tests do not execute third-party player scripts; saved Bilibili references still expose a usable external video link.
Strict review fix: browser production search now uses a small fetch-backed Bilibili adapter by default, while deterministic E2E opts into the fixture adapter through an explicit browser test flag.

Strict review fix: reference E2E now verifies recording independence through the real Start recording / Stop recording UI and sheet recording service with a synthetic microphone stream, not the harness flag.
DB cleanup helper duplication was left local to this E2E because there is no existing shared helper; extracting common helpers would touch unrelated E2E ownership outside this review fix.
changedAreas: src/domain/reference adds reference types, local audio validation, Bilibili URL parsing via URL, search result normalization, volume clamping, and zod-backed persisted record validation.

src/services/reference adds the reference service boundary for local audio add, Bilibili URL save, Bilibili search result save, adapter-backed search, active reference lookup, artifacts, and subscriptions.
src/infrastructure/reference adds Dexie persistence, browser local audio decode inspection, browser local audio playback boundary, and browser service composition.
src/infrastructure/bilibili adds DeterministicBilibiliSearchAdapter for stable automated Bilibili search flows.
src/infrastructure/bilibili/fetch-bilibili-search-adapter.ts adds a production fetch-backed Bilibili search adapter with API response normalization and error handling.
src/components/sheet-practice/reference adds the Sheet Practice reference panel with local audio, Bilibili search, Bilibili URL, active reference, playback, volume, embed/link, and error states.
src/components/sheet-practice/reference/reference-panel.tsx now renders Bilibili references as an external-link fallback instead of loading the third-party iframe during practice.
src/components/sheet-practice/viewer/sheet-viewer-experience.tsx composes the reference panel into Sheet Practice with a bounded viewer/reference row and existing controls below.

tests/unit/reference-domain.test.ts and tests/unit/reference-service.test.ts cover validation, URL parsing, volume clamp, service persistence, artifacts, search, URL save, sheet scoping, and error results.
tests/unit/bilibili-search-adapter.test.ts covers fetch-backed Bilibili search normalization, query parameters, and failure handling.

tests/e2e/reference-system.spec.ts covers the real browser reference workflow with local audio fixture generation, persistence, Bilibili search fixture, URL paste, independence, resize, and console capture.
knownRisks: Bilibili production search is fetch-backed against Bilibili's public web search endpoint and may fail because of network, CORS, rate limits, or third-party response changes; failures return a visible error.
Bilibili inline iframe playback is intentionally not rendered in v0 to avoid third-party script noise and coupling; the saved reference always includes a safe external Bilibili link fallback.
Local audio decode/playback depends on browser AudioContext and HTMLMediaElement support; unsupported or bad files show clear errors and are not saved as playable references.
Reference persistence is local IndexedDB only; cloud sync, download, extraction, sync, A-B loop, playback speed, offset, waveform comparison, and analysis remain out of scope.
